title: xylia xylocarpa var. xylocarpa
intro: Xylia xylocarpa var. xylocarpa, also known as the 'katah', is a plant species in the Fabaceae family, native to tropical Asia. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its seeds, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Xylia xylocarpa var. xylocarpa include its potential use in treating fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its ability to lower blood sugar levels. Xylia xylocarpa var. xylocarpa is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its seeds can also be dried and consumed as a powder.
